Velvoix

From signal to clear care action

Velvoix helps healthcare organisations turn button calls, voice input and other care-related signals into explainable, prioritised and auditable care workflow.

Schedule a pilot conversation See how it works

Built for care environments where clarity, follow-up and reconstruction matter.

Explainable

From incoming signal to understandable follow-up

Auditable

Traceable status flow and reconstruction

Modular

Designed to fit existing care environments

Care workflow in context

Calm on the surface, clarity in follow-up.

Velvoix flow

From trigger to controllable follow-up

Public conceptual
01

Start

Signal received

02

Layer

Context added

03

Layer

Triage explained

04

Outcome

Workflow active

Brand principle

Not a loose AI layer, but a platform for clear care action.

Velvoix does not present signals as isolated alerts, but as explainable workflow with context, follow-up and reconstruction.

The challenge

When a signal is not yet a clear care action

Many systems can register that something happened, but they do not sufficiently support the next step: what is likely going on, how urgent is it, what follow-up fits the situation and how can the full sequence later be reconstructed properly?

  • More clarity on what requires attention
  • Better prioritisation of follow-up
  • Less interpretation burden on staff
  • Better reconstruction of what happened

Chain difference

Not from alert to interpretation, but from signal to controllable workflow

The value of Velvoix sits in the full chain. Not only surfacing that something happened, but structuring signals into a workable and explainable outcome.

Traditional

Traditional chain

1 Signal
2 Alert
3 Manual interpretation

Velvoix

Velvoix chain

01

Signal

02

Context

03

Triage

04

Event

05

Workflow

06

Escalation

07

Reconstruction

How it works

A clear chain from input to follow-up

Publicly understandable, operationally useful and designed to stay calm under care pressure.

Input

1

Button calls, voice input and other care-related triggers enter the flow as the starting point for follow-up.

Interpretation

2

Velvoix helps structure context, urgency and a logical course of action so a signal becomes easier to understand.

Operational follow-up

3

The outcome becomes a workable event for dashboard handling, status flow, ownership and escalation.

Audit and oversight

4

Actions, status progression and follow-up remain traceable for evaluation, quality improvement and operational oversight.

Why Velvoix is different

System differences that matter operationally

Velvoix is not positioned as a notification product, but as a controlled chain for triage, follow-up and reconstruction.

Not a notification layer

1

Velvoix does not let signals circulate as isolated alerts, but turns them into workable events with context and follow-up.

Explainable triage

2

Not black-box scoring, but a clear combination of urgency, context and recommended action in care language.

Event truth for workflow and audit

3

One operational truth for status, ownership, escalation and reconstruction.

Product snapshots

Selected proof layers, not a product tour

Not full dashboard pages, but compact product fragments that show how Velvoix makes operational clarity, explainability and control visible.

operations

Events worklist

Urgency, resident and follow-up remain directly scannable in a compact work card.

explainability

Event detail

Why and recommended action are structured into a calm, explainable detail view.

context

Resident context

Relevant resident context and care signals without visual noise or interpretation burden.

control

Management and control

The management layer stays calm, scannable and operationally controlled as well.

Pilot

Interested in a pilot?

Velvoix is designed for healthcare organisations that want more than incoming alerts. It is built for teams that want clearer follow-up, better prioritisation and stronger operational control.

Schedule a pilot conversation Request information

Pilot focus

  • Nursing homes
  • Long-term care environments
  • Organisations looking for greater operational clarity
